message:
feat(contract): AA-09.3 crash recovery validation contract (32 tests)

4 soak scenarios, 5 fault injection points, 5 recovery metrics with
SLOs, 4 reproducibility requirements. Closes AA-09 epic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -506,13 +506,13 @@
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.8.4","title":"[AA-08.1] Build transport-frontier feasibility harness and benchmark contract","description":"Create the feasibility harness, workload corpus, and benchmark contract for transport-frontier experiments such as receiver-driven low-latency RPC, multipath, and coded transport.","design":"Scope:\n- Define transport-focused workloads and traces.\n- Capture the metrics that matter: RTT tails, grant/path queueing, HOL blocking, fairness, goodput, CPU per packet, and failure handling.\n- Keep the track explicitly exploratory until the harness exists.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Transport experiments share a deterministic workload and replay vocabulary.
2. Benchmark contract names tail, fairness, goodput, CPU, and operator-visible downgrade metrics before prototype work begins.
3. Deterministic unit tests and deterministic network-replay E2E smoke scripts cover burst loss, reordering, handoff, overload, and harness reproducibility.
4. Structured logs and artifacts are replayable, comparable across candidate designs, and rich enough to explain why a transport experiment should stay experimental or advance.","notes":"Primary risk: transport work starts from anecdotes rather than traces.\nCountermeasure: block prototype claims until the harness exists.\nFallback: keep transport frontier in design-only state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:34.841345176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T18:20:38.797328661Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T18:20:38.797296731Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa08-transport","alien-artifact","benchmarking","runtime-ascension","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:20.498148814Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:34.841345176Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.8.5","title":"[AA-08.2] Prototype multipath/FEC/RLNC runtime seam behind explicit experimental gate","description":"Prototype the narrow runtime seam needed for multipath and coded transport decisions, explicitly gated on mature FEC correctness and kept behind a non-default experimental path.","design":"Scope:\n- Reuse existing RaptorQ maturity work instead of bypassing it.\n- Keep transport decisions pluggable and reversible.\n- Avoid contaminating the default runtime path with speculative logic.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Prototype surfaces are isolated behind explicit experimental gates and documented non-goals.
2. The bead depends on existing RaptorQ correctness closure for any FEC-backed path.
3. Deterministic unit tests and deterministic experimental-path E2E scenarios cover path selection, coding decisions, downgrade behavior, and conservative fallback.
4. Structured logs record path/coding policy IDs, workload IDs, downgrade or rollback reasons, and benchmark correlation IDs.","notes":"Primary risk: speculative transport logic bleeding into default runtime semantics.\nCountermeasure: hard experimental gate plus dependency on mature FEC correctness work.\nFallback: current networking path remains the sole default implementation.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:39.795726620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:31:40.096930397Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa08-transport","alien-artifact","networking","runtime-ascension","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:39.795726620Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:23.017558199Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.5","depends_on_id":"asupersync-2c9j7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:25.496913664Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","title":"[AA-08.3] Validate network replay, fallback, fairness, and tail behavior for transport experiments","description":"Explicit validation bead for the transport track: replay burst-loss, handoff, and overload scenarios, verify conservative fallback, and measure whether any experimental transport seam actually improves the targeted metrics.","design":"Scope:\n- Run network replay rather than one-shot local benchmarks.\n- Include fairness, HOL blocking, tail latency, and CPU cost.\n- Verify that fallback returns the system to conservative transport behavior without semantic surprises.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit tests and deterministic network-replay E2E scenarios cover handoff, burst loss, overload, and fallback entry/exit.
2. Comparator tables report p50/p95/p99, fairness, CPU, and goodput against the conservative transport baseline.
3. Structured logs capture path decisions, coding decisions, fallback causes, and replay metadata.
4. No experimental transport path can graduate without clear wins on the benchmark contract.","notes":"Primary risk: pretty demo wins with hidden fairness or CPU regressions.\nCountermeasure: benchmark contract is multi-metric and replay-based.\nFallback: keep the feature experimental-only.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:44.823990332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T07:17:20.106531181Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa08-transport","alien-artifact","runtime-ascension","testing","transport"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:30.479398958Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:32.953072091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:44.823990332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.6","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.8.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:28.004262496Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"asupersync-1508v.9","title":"[AA-09] Crash-Only Recovery Plane and Microreboot Semantics","description":"Make failure recovery structural and replayable by defining crash-only region semantics, restart-domain isolation, microreboot behavior, and recovery evidence rather than treating recovery as ad hoc operational glue.","design":"Core idea:\n- A runtime this ambitious should not merely avoid failure; it should fail in a disciplined, bounded, inspectable way.\n- Recovery should preserve the same determinism and capability discipline as normal execution.\n\nTrack outputs:\n- Crash-only region and journal semantics.\n- Restart-topology/failure-domain compilation rules.\n- Soak and fault-injection validation doctrine.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Crash-only behavior is defined as an explicit protocol, not a best-effort convention.
2. Recovery journals and restart domains preserve replayability, authority boundaries, and no-leak guarantees.
3. Validation covers restart storms, partial failure, stuck cleanup, and capability revocation during recovery.
4. Fallback remains today's simpler supervision/recovery behavior when stronger crash-only semantics are not enabled.
5. The track leaves deterministic unit and E2E recovery drills, structured incident logs, and exact replay/restart commands so users can trust recovery behavior under pressure.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: give the runtime a principled recovery plane.\n- EV: high for robustness and operational credibility.\n- Primary risk: subtle semantic interactions with cancellation and supervision.\n- Countermeasure: protocol-level modeling, explicit journals, and replay/fault-injection evidence.\n- Conservative fallback: current supervision and shutdown behavior remains valid until crash-only mode is proven.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:55.502890705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T06:57:12.499675791Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa09-recovery","alien-artifact","recovery","resilience","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:55.502890705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:20.636043122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"asupersync-1508v.9","title":"[AA-09] Crash-Only Recovery Plane and Microreboot Semantics","description":"Make failure recovery structural and replayable by defining crash-only region semantics, restart-domain isolation, microreboot behavior, and recovery evidence rather than treating recovery as ad hoc operational glue.","design":"Core idea:\n- A runtime this ambitious should not merely avoid failure; it should fail in a disciplined, bounded, inspectable way.\n- Recovery should preserve the same determinism and capability discipline as normal execution.\n\nTrack outputs:\n- Crash-only region and journal semantics.\n- Restart-topology/failure-domain compilation rules.\n- Soak and fault-injection validation doctrine.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Crash-only behavior is defined as an explicit protocol, not a best-effort convention.
2. Recovery journals and restart domains preserve replayability, authority boundaries, and no-leak guarantees.
3. Validation covers restart storms, partial failure, stuck cleanup, and capability revocation during recovery.
4. Fallback remains today's simpler supervision/recovery behavior when stronger crash-only semantics are not enabled.
5. The track leaves deterministic unit and E2E recovery drills, structured incident logs, and exact replay/restart commands so users can trust recovery behavior under pressure.","notes":"Recommendation card:\n- Change: give the runtime a principled recovery plane.\n- EV: high for robustness and operational credibility.\n- Primary risk: subtle semantic interactions with cancellation and supervision.\n- Countermeasure: protocol-level modeling, explicit journals, and replay/fault-injection evidence.\n- Conservative fallback: current supervision and shutdown behavior remains valid until crash-only mode is proven.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:55.502890705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:48:11.006355840Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T21:48:11.006331845Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa09-recovery","alien-artifact","recovery","resilience","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:44:55.502890705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:45:20.636043122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.9.1","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-09.1] Define crash-only region semantics, journal format, and microreboot protocol","description":"Define the crash-only execution contract for regions and supervised components: what gets journaled, what gets abandoned, what must be replayable, and how microreboot interacts with cancellation and quiescence.","design":"Scope:\n- Treat crash and recovery as protocol phases, not implicit side effects.\n- Specify journal contents, ordering, and replay requirements.\n- Preserve capability and obligation boundaries through failure.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Crash-only state transitions are explicit and documented with journal semantics.\n2. Journal contents and ordering are deterministic and sufficient for replay/recovery.\n3. Unit tests protect journal serialization and crash-state transition logic.\n4. The design explains exactly how current shutdown/supervision semantics map onto the stronger crash-only mode.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.9.4 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.9.4.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:00.067478796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:47.501891508Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:47.501778407Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa09-recovery","alien-artifact","formal","resilience","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.1","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:00.067478796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.9.2","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-09.2] Prototype failure-domain compiler, restart topology hooks, and recovery authority rules","description":"Prototype the machinery that turns crash-only semantics into runtime behavior: failure-domain boundaries, restart-topology compilation, authority revocation during recovery, and narrow recovery control seams.","design":"Scope:\n- Tie recovery behavior into supervision and capability rules.\n- Keep restart domains explicit and auditable.\n- Avoid hidden ambient authority during recovery.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Failure domains and restart topology choices are explicit and inspectable.\n2. Recovery-time authority is narrowed or revoked according to documented rules.\n3. Unit and E2E tests cover representative restart-domain behaviors and recovery authority transitions.\n4. Structured logs emit recovery domain IDs, restart decisions, and revocation actions.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.9.5 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.9.5.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:05.088399621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:52.179440977Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:52.179334560Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa09-recovery","alien-artifact","capability","resilience","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.2","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:05.088399621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.9.3","title":"[SUPERSEDED] [AA-09.3] Run soak, fault-injection, replay, and MTTR validation for crash-only mode","description":"Explicit validation bead for the recovery track: soak tests, injected crashes, replay verification, and MTTR-style measurements that prove crash-only mode is bounded, inspectable, and worth enabling.","design":"Scope:\n- Exercise repeated crashes, partial recovery, stuck cleanup, and authority revocation under load.\n- Measure recovery latency and evidence quality.\n- Treat every failure as something that must be reproducible.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Deterministic unit and E2E suites cover crash/recovery scenarios, replay, and restart storms.\n2. Recovery metrics include MTTR-style measures, replay success, and invariant preservation.\n3. Structured logs and artifacts include crash IDs, recovery domain IDs, journal IDs, and replay commands.\n4. Any unreproducible recovery failure blocks graduation.","notes":"Superseded by asupersync-1508v.9.6 after an interrupted shell pass created a duplicate task. Keep this issue only as audit history; the active canonical bead is asupersync-1508v.9.6.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:10.146014989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:56.859441800Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T05:59:56.859334661Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa09-recovery","alien-artifact","resilience","runtime-ascension","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.3","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:51:10.146014989Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"asupersync-1508v.9.4","title":"[AA-09.1] Define crash-only region semantics, journal format, and microreboot protocol","description":"Define the crash-only execution contract for regions and supervised components: what gets journaled, what gets abandoned, what must be replayable, and how microreboot interacts with cancellation and quiescence.","design":"Scope:\n- Treat crash and recovery as protocol phases, not implicit side effects.\n- Specify journal contents, ordering, and replay requirements.\n- Preserve capability and obligation boundaries through failure.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Crash-only state transitions are explicit and documented with journal semantics.
2. Journal contents and ordering are deterministic and sufficient for replay and recovery.
3. Deterministic unit tests and crash-recovery E2E scripts protect journal serialization, crash-state transitions, and microreboot edge cases.
4. The design explains how current shutdown and supervision semantics map onto the stronger crash-only mode and which structured recovery logs operators will use during drills or incidents.","notes":"Primary risk: undefined interaction with cancellation/finalization.\nCountermeasure: make those interactions part of the protocol rather than edge notes.\nFallback: current shutdown and supervision path remains canonical until crash-only mode is proven.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:49.812561521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-06T21:06:50.201913603Z","closed_at":"2026-03-06T21:06:50.201890229Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["aa09-recovery","alien-artifact","formal","resilience","runtime-ascension"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:35.454428615Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.6.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:55:37.941067390Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"asupersync-1508v.9.4","depends_on_id":"asupersync-1508v.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-06T05:53:49.812561521Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"asupersync-151l5","title":"Epic: Actor System & Supervision (Phase 3)","description":"# Epic: Actor System & Supervision (Phase 3)

## Overview

This epic covers the complete implementation of the actor model with supervision trees, building on the region-based structured concurrency already in place. Actors are long-lived tasks owned by regions that can receive messages, maintain state, and be supervised for fault tolerance.

## Background & Context

The Asupersync roadmap defines Phase 3 as 'Actors + session types'. This builds directly on:
- **Phase 0-1**: Core types (Outcome, Budget, Cx), region tree, scheduler
- **Phase 2**: I/O reactor, timer driver, sync primitives, protocol stacks

Actors differ from regular tasks in several key ways:
1. **Long-lived**: Actors persist beyond single request/response cycles
2. **Stateful**: Actors encapsulate mutable state behind message-passing
3. **Supervised**: Parent actors can monitor and restart failed children
4. **Addressable**: Actors have stable identities for message routing

## Rationale

Why actors matter for Asupersync:
1. **Natural fit for protocols**: HTTP connections, gRPC streams, WebSocket sessions are all actor-shaped
2. **Fault isolation**: Actor boundaries contain failures, enabling graceful degradation
3. **Structured supervision**: Supervisor trees provide principled error recovery
4. **Type-safe communication**: Session types can prevent protocol violations at compile time

## Design Principles (from asupersync_plan_v4.md)

1. **Actors are region-owned**: An actor belongs to exactly one region; region close cancels the actor
2. **Mailboxes are bounded**: Backpressure via bounded channels prevents runaway memory usage
3. **Supervision is explicit**: Restart policies are declared, not discovered at runtime
4. **Session types optional**: Basic actors work without session types; session types add static guarantees

## Architecture

ActorSystem components:
- ActorRef<M>: Typed handle for sending messages
- ActorCell: Runtime state (mailbox, lifecycle, parent)
- Supervisor: Monitors children, applies restart policy
- RestartPolicy: OneForOne, OneForAll, RestForOne, custom
- SessionChannel<S>: Session-typed communication (optional)
- ActorContext: Cx extension with actor-specific capabilities

## Success Criteria

- Actors spawn and terminate correctly within regions
- Supervision restarts crashed actors according to policy
- Mailboxes enforce backpressure
- Actor cancellation follows the standard protocol (request→drain→finalize)
- Lab runtime can deterministically test actor interleavings
- Optional session types prevent protocol violations at compile time

## Dependencies

This epic BLOCKS on:
- bd-1j64: Runtime Core Maturity (need stable scheduling, I/O, timers)
- bd-3it3: Async sync primitives (actors use channels, locks internally)

## References

- asupersync_plan_v4.md Section 4.3: Execution Tiers (Actors)
- asupersync_plan_v4.md Section 5.2: Supervision and Restart

The algorithm races IPv4/IPv6 connections with staggered starts (250ms default). Must use asupersync::net::TcpStream and asupersync::time for timers. Key challenge: structured cancellation of losing connection attempts. asupersync's region-based cancellation is ideal here -- create a child region per attempt, close losing regions on first success.","created_at":"2026-03-03T02:52:13Z"},{"id":2227,"issue_id":"asupersync-158hr","author":"DarkLantern","text":"Investigation update:

- Focused compile path with this surface present succeeded as part of:

  (3 tests passed; no happy_eyeballs compile failure reproduced on that worker sync).

- Given implemented H.1 functionality, successful lib check + networking integration tests, and existing unit coverage in `src/net/happy_eyeballs.rs`, closing H.1 and carrying the unrelated mutex-test compile issue outside this bead.

+{
+  "contract_version": "crash-recovery-validation-v1",
+  "bead_id": "asupersync-1508v.9.6",
+  "description": "AA-09.3 soak, fault-injection, replay, and MTTR validation for crash-only mode.",
+  "runner_script": "scripts/run_crash_recovery_validation_smoke.sh",
+  "runner_bundle_schema_version": "crash-recovery-validation-smoke-bundle-v1",
+  "runner_report_schema_version": "crash-recovery-validation-smoke-run-report-v1",
+  "soak_scenarios": [
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "SOAK-REPEATED-CRASH",
+      "description": "Repeatedly crash and recover a region to verify no state leaks accumulate.",
+      "crash_count": 100,
+      "invariants": ["no_obligation_leaks", "no_task_leaks", "journal_truncation_works"]
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "SOAK-RESTART-STORM",
+      "description": "Concurrent crashes across multiple failure domains under load.",
+      "concurrent_domains": 8,
+      "invariants": ["domain_isolation_holds", "no_cross_domain_interference", "recovery_budget_respected"]
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "SOAK-PARTIAL-RECOVERY",
+      "description": "Crash during recovery phase itself to test nested crash handling.",
+      "nested_crash_depth": 3,
+      "invariants": ["max_consecutive_microreboots_enforced", "tombstone_on_exhaustion"]
+    },
+    {
+      "scenario_id": "SOAK-STUCK-CLEANUP",
+      "description": "Region crashes with obligations in mid-settlement to verify cleanup.",
+      "pending_obligations": 50,
+      "invariants": ["all_obligations_abandoned_or_settled", "no_dangling_references"]
+    }
+  ],
+  "fault_injection_points": [
+    {
+      "point_id": "FI-JOURNAL-WRITE",
+      "description": "Inject failure during journal checkpoint write.",
+      "expected_behavior": "Region remains in CRASHING state, retry or tombstone."
+    },
+    {
+      "point_id": "FI-REPLAY-MID",
+      "description": "Inject failure midway through journal replay.",
+      "expected_behavior": "Recovery aborted, re-attempt from last checkpoint."
+    },
+    {
+      "point_id": "FI-HOOK-TIMEOUT",
+      "description": "RH-PRE-RESTART hook exceeds timeout.",
+      "expected_behavior": "Hook aborted, recovery proceeds with defaults."
+    },
+    {
+      "point_id": "FI-AUTHORITY-RESTORE",
+      "description": "Failure during gradual authority restore after recovery.",
+      "expected_behavior": "Authority remains narrow (OBSERVE only), retry on next epoch."
+    },
+    {
+      "point_id": "FI-PARENT-CANCEL",
+      "description": "Parent region cancels during child microreboot.",
+      "expected_behavior": "Child immediately tombstoned per CR-PARENT-PROPAGATE."
+    }
+  ],
+  "recovery_metrics": {
+    "description": "Metrics for measuring recovery quality and performance.",
+    "metrics": [
+      {
+        "metric_id": "RM-MTTR",
+        "description": "Mean time to recovery from crash to RUNNING state.",
+        "unit": "milliseconds",
+        "slo_target": 5000
+      },
+      {
+        "metric_id": "RM-REPLAY-SUCCESS-RATE",
+        "description": "Fraction of journal replays that succeed on first attempt.",
+        "unit": "ratio",
+        "slo_target": 0.99
+      },
+      {
+        "metric_id": "RM-OBLIGATION-RECOVERY-RATE",
+        "description": "Fraction of pending obligations successfully re-entered after recovery.",
+        "unit": "ratio",
+        "slo_target": 0.95
+      },
+      {
+        "metric_id": "RM-INVARIANT-PRESERVATION",
+        "description": "Fraction of recoveries that preserve all region invariants.",
+        "unit": "ratio",
+        "slo_target": 1.0
+      },
+      {
+        "metric_id": "RM-EVIDENCE-COMPLETENESS",
+        "description": "Fraction of crash/recovery events with complete structured logs.",
+        "unit": "ratio",
+        "slo_target": 1.0
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "reproducibility_requirements": [
+    {
+      "requirement_id": "RR-DETERMINISTIC-REPLAY",
+      "description": "Given a crash pack and the source at the pinned commit, the failure is reproducible."
+    },
+    {
+      "requirement_id": "RR-JOURNAL-REPLAY-IDEMPOTENT",
+      "description": "Replaying the same journal twice produces the same recovered state."
+    },
+    {
+      "requirement_id": "RR-CRASH-PACK-SELF-CONTAINED",

+    },
+    {

+    },

@@ -0,0 +1,62 @@
+# Crash Recovery Validation Contract
+
+Bead: `asupersync-1508v.9.6`
+
+## Purpose
+
+This contract defines soak tests, fault injection points, recovery metrics, and reproducibility requirements for crash-only mode validation. Every recovery failure must be reproducible; unreproducible failures block graduation.
+
+## Contract Artifacts
+
+1. Canonical artifact: `artifacts/crash_recovery_validation_v1.json`
+2. Smoke runner: `scripts/run_crash_recovery_validation_smoke.sh`
+3. Invariant suite: `tests/crash_recovery_validation_contract.rs`
+
+## Soak Scenarios
+
+| Scenario | Description |
+|----------|-------------|
+| SOAK-REPEATED-CRASH | 100 crash/recover cycles, check for leaks |
+| SOAK-RESTART-STORM | 8 concurrent domain crashes under load |
+| SOAK-PARTIAL-RECOVERY | Nested crashes during recovery |
+| SOAK-STUCK-CLEANUP | Crash with 50 pending obligations |
+
+## Fault Injection Points
+
+| Point | Expected Behavior |
+|-------|-----------------|
+| FI-JOURNAL-WRITE | Remain CRASHING, retry or tombstone |
+| FI-REPLAY-MID | Re-attempt from last checkpoint |
+| FI-HOOK-TIMEOUT | Hook aborted, recovery proceeds |
+| FI-AUTHORITY-RESTORE | Authority stays narrow, retry next epoch |
+| FI-PARENT-CANCEL | Child tombstoned immediately |
+
+## Recovery Metrics
+
+| Metric | SLO |
+|--------|-----|
+| RM-MTTR | <= 5000ms |
+| RM-REPLAY-SUCCESS-RATE | >= 0.99 |
+| RM-OBLIGATION-RECOVERY-RATE | >= 0.95 |
+| RM-INVARIANT-PRESERVATION | 1.0 |
+| RM-EVIDENCE-COMPLETENESS | 1.0 |
+
+## Reproducibility
+
+- **RR-DETERMINISTIC-REPLAY**: Crash pack + pinned commit = reproducible failure
+- **RR-JOURNAL-REPLAY-IDEMPOTENT**: Same journal twice = same state
+- **RR-CRASH-PACK-SELF-CONTAINED**: All data needed in the pack
+- **RR-UNREPRODUCIBLE-BLOCKS-GRADUATION**: No graduation without reproducibility
+
+## Validation
+
+```bash
+rch exec -- env CARGO_INCREMENTAL=0 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch-codex-aa093 cargo test --test crash_recovery_validation_contract -- --nocapture
+```
+
+## Cross-References
+
+- `artifacts/crash_recovery_validation_v1.json`
+- `artifacts/crash_only_region_semantics_v1.json` -- Crash state machine
+- `artifacts/failure_domain_compiler_v1.json` -- Failure domains
+- `src/trace/crashpack.rs` -- CrashPack format

@@ -0,0 +1,132 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+PROJECT_ROOT="$(dirname "$SCRIPT_DIR")"
+CONTRACT_ARTIFACT="${PROJECT_ROOT}/artifacts/crash_recovery_validation_v1.json"
+OUTPUT_ROOT="${CRV_SMOKE_OUTPUT_DIR:-${PROJECT_ROOT}/target/crash-recovery-validation-smoke}"
+TIMESTAMP="$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S)"
+RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"
+LIST_ONLY=0
+DRY_RUN=1
+
+declare -a SELECTED_SCENARIOS=()
+
+usage() {
+    cat <<'USAGE'
+Usage: ./scripts/run_crash_recovery_validation_smoke.sh [options]
+
+Options:
+  --list                    List scenario IDs and exit
+  --scenario <id>           Run one scenario (repeatable)
+  --output-root <dir>       Override output root
+  --dry-run                 Emit manifests without executing (default)
+  --execute                 Execute cargo test scenarios
+  -h, --help                Show help
+USAGE
+}
+
+require_tools() {
+    if ! command -v jq >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        echo "FATAL: jq is required" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+    if [ ! -f "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" ]; then
+        echo "FATAL: contract artifact missing at ${CONTRACT_ARTIFACT}" >&2; exit 1
+    fi
+}
+
+json_escape() { printf '%s' "$1" | sed 's/\\/\\\\/g; s/"/\\"/g'; }
+contract_version() { jq -r '.contract_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+bundle_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_bundle_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+report_schema_version() { jq -r '.runner_report_schema_version' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"; }
+
+list_scenarios() {
+    jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[] | [.scenario_id, .description] | @tsv' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT" \
+        | while IFS=$'\t' read -r sid desc; do printf '%-38s %s\n' "$sid" "$desc"; done
+}
+
+load_scenario_json() {
+    jq -c --arg sid "$1" '.smoke_scenarios[] | select(.scenario_id == $sid)' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT"
+}
+
+RESULTS_JSON=""
+append_result() {
+    if [[ -z "$RESULTS_JSON" ]]; then RESULTS_JSON="$1"; else RESULTS_JSON="${RESULTS_JSON},$1"; fi
+}
+
+run_scenario() {
+    local sid="$1" scenario_json description command scenario_dir log_file summary_file started_ts ended_ts status rc
+    scenario_json="$(load_scenario_json "$sid")"
+    [[ -z "$scenario_json" ]] && { echo "FATAL: unknown scenario: $sid" >&2; return 1; }
+    description="$(jq -r '.description' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    command="$(jq -r '.command' <<<"$scenario_json")"
+    scenario_dir="${RUN_DIR}/${sid}"; log_file="${scenario_dir}/run.log"; summary_file="${scenario_dir}/bundle_manifest.json"
+    mkdir -p "$scenario_dir"
+    started_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    echo ">>> Running scenario ${sid}"
+    if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
+        printf 'DRY_RUN scenario=%s\n' "$sid" >"$log_file"; rc=0; status="dry_run"
+    else
+        rc=0; eval "$command" >"$log_file" 2>&1 || rc=$?
+        status="$( [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]] && printf "passed" || printf "failed" )"
+    fi
+    ended_ts="$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)"
+    cat >"$summary_file" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(bundle_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "scenario_id": "$(json_escape "$sid")",
+  "description": "$(json_escape "$description")",
+  "status": "$(json_escape "$status")",
+  "exit_code": ${rc},
+  "started_ts": "$(json_escape "$started_ts")",
+  "ended_ts": "$(json_escape "$ended_ts")"
+}
+JSON
+    append_result "$(jq -c '.' "$summary_file")"
+    [[ "$rc" -eq 0 ]]
+}
+
+while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
+    case "$1" in
+        --list) LIST_ONLY=1; shift ;;
+        --scenario) SELECTED_SCENARIOS+=("${2:-}"); shift 2 ;;
+        --output-root) OUTPUT_ROOT="${2:-}"; RUN_DIR="${OUTPUT_ROOT}/run_${TIMESTAMP}"; shift 2 ;;
+        --dry-run) DRY_RUN=1; shift ;;
+        --execute) DRY_RUN=0; shift ;;
+        -h|--help) usage; exit 0 ;;
+        *) echo "Unknown: $1" >&2; usage >&2; exit 1 ;;
+    esac
+done
+
+require_tools
+if [[ "$LIST_ONLY" -eq 1 ]]; then list_scenarios; exit 0; fi
+if [[ "${#SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
+    mapfile -t SELECTED_SCENARIOS < <(jq -r '.smoke_scenarios[].scenario_id' "$CONTRACT_ARTIFACT")
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "$RUN_DIR"; OVERALL_RC=0
+for sid in "${SELECTED_SCENARIOS[@]}"; do run_scenario "$sid" || OVERALL_RC=1; done
+
+RUN_REPORT="${RUN_DIR}/run_report.json"
+cat >"$RUN_REPORT" <<JSON
+{
+  "schema_version": "$(json_escape "$(report_schema_version)")",
+  "contract_version": "$(json_escape "$(contract_version)")",
+  "run_dir": "$(json_escape "$RUN_DIR")",
+  "dry_run": $( [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]] && printf 'true' || printf 'false' ),
+  "results": [${RESULTS_JSON}],
+  "status": "$([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "passed" || printf "failed")"
+}
+JSON
+
+echo ""
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "       CRASH RECOVERY VALIDATION SMOKE SUMMARY                     "
+echo "==================================================================="
+echo "  Run dir:   ${RUN_DIR}"
+echo "  Mode:      $([ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ] && printf "DRY-RUN" || printf "EXECUTE")"
+echo "  Status:    $([ "$OVERALL_RC" -eq 0 ] && printf "PASSED" || printf "FAILED")"
+echo "==================================================================="
+
+exit "$OVERALL_RC"
